description: False-colour satellite image of the Araslövssjöområdet Natura 2000 site in Sweden, captured by SPOT-5 at 2.5 m resolution using a 1/2/3 band combination on 2011-06-28. The image source is CNES 2011©, distributed by Airbus DS/Spot Image. The Natura 2000 site boundary is depicted by white outlines, encompassing a diverse landscape. Within these boundaries, two distinct land cover types are labelled with CORINE Land Cover (CLC) codes: "411" identifies an area of Inland marshes, appearing as mottled reddish-purple and green vegetation. "412" identifies an area of Saline marshes, characterized by a yellowish-green colour. Surrounding the marsh areas are dense forests and woodlands, depicted in deep red, and a network of river channels and water bodies, shown in light blue/cyan. The image illustrates examples of "Inland marshes" interpretation rules, contrasting different wetland types within the protected area.
